title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nemoralis
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. nemoralis, also known as the woodland columbine, is a subspecies of the columbine plant, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers and fern-like foliage. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with potential applications in wound healing and soothing skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including lowering blood pressure and improving respiratory function, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the woodland columbine is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
